questionSelectionRules.name: Core - Basic Identity
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include basic identity questions as foundation for any IR-2 interview

actions.reason: Basic identity verification forms the foundation for confirming beneficiary eligibility and age requirements under IR-2 category

questionSelectionRules.name: Core - Petitioner Information
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include petitioner identification and status questions

actions.reason: Petitioner verification confirms legal sponsor status, citizenship, and parent identity for IR-2 filing

questionSelectionRules.name: Core - Parent-Child Relationship
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include relationship strength and communication questions

actions.reason: Relationship verification is critical for establishing genuine parent-child bond and family reunification intent under IR-2

questionSelectionRules.name: Core - Living Arrangements in USA
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include US residence and household planning questions

actions.reason: Post-arrival living arrangements confirm practical integration plan and parent-child household stability

questionSelectionRules.name: Core - Immigration Intent
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include family reunification and future plans questions

actions.reason: Immigration intent assessment ensures genuine desire for family reunification, core purpose of IR-2 visa category

questionSelectionRules.name: Special Case - Stepchild
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary's relationship type is Stepchild

actions.reason: Stepchild status requires detailed verification of step-parent marriage timing, legitimacy of relationship, and family bond strength before age 18

questionSelectionRules.name: Special Case - Adopted Child
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary's relationship type is Adopted

actions.reason: Adoption requires verification of legal adoption documents, timing of adoption (before age 16), consent from biological parents, and legitimacy of parent-child relationship

questionSelectionRules.name: Separated Living - Not Currently with Parent
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary does not currently live with petitioner

actions.reason: Separated living requires detailed explanation of guardian arrangements, relationship maintenance during separation, other parent consent, and reason for non-cohabitation

questionSelectionRules.name: Strong Parent-Child Communication
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent and child communicate daily or several times per week

actions.reason: Frequent communication demonstrates active ongoing relationship engagement and emotional closeness despite physical separation if applicable

questionSelectionRules.name: Weak Parent-Child Communication
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent and child communicate occasionally or rarely

actions.reason: Infrequent communication raises concerns about relationship strength and requires detailed explanation of communication barriers and genuine commitment to family reunification

questionSelectionRules.name: Relationship Evidence - Photos
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary has photos with parent

actions.reason: Physical evidence of shared moments supports relationship authenticity and demonstrates family bonding across visits or periods together

questionSelectionRules.name: Missing Relationship Evidence - No Photos
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary has no photos with parent

actions.reason: Absence of photographic evidence requires alternative proof of relationship such as travel records, communication logs, or detailed family history

questionSelectionRules.name: Financial Support from Petitioner
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent has provided financial support

actions.reason: Financial support demonstrates parental responsibility, financial capacity, and commitment to child welfare during separation

questionSelectionRules.name: No Financial Support from Petitioner
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent has not provided financial support

actions.reason: Lack of financial support requires explanation of financial arrangements, reason for absence of support, and petitioner's income for I-864 Affidavit capacity

questionSelectionRules.name: Previous US Visit Experience
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary has visited United States before

actions.reason: Previous US visits verify immigration compliance history, familiarity with US, and opportunity for parent-child bonding during visits

questionSelectionRules.name: Visa Refusal History
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary has been denied a US visa previously

actions.reason: Visa refusal history requires detailed explanation of refusal grounds, what circumstances have changed, evidence supporting revised eligibility, and commitment to compliance

questionSelectionRules.name: Criminal History
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary has criminal record

actions.reason: Criminal history requires full disclosure, detailed explanation of circumstances, evidence of rehabilitation, and assurance of future lawful conduct

questionSelectionRules.name: Education Plans - Child Will Study
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary plans to continue studies in USA

actions.reason: Education plans demonstrate positive post-arrival engagement, integration pathway, and legitimate purpose for family reunification in United States

questionSelectionRules.name: Education Plans - Child Will Not Study
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary does not plan to study in USA

actions.reason: Absence of education plans requires detailed explanation of alternative post-arrival activities, employment prospects, and long-term integration strategy

questionSelectionRules.name: Family Structure - Has Siblings
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary has siblings

actions.reason: Sibling relationships clarify family structure, remaining ties in home country, and family separation implications of immigration

questionSelectionRules.name: Family Structure - Only Child
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary has no siblings

actions.reason: Only child status emphasizes importance of parent-child relationship and demonstrates limited family ties in home country

questionSelectionRules.name: High-Risk: Separated + Weak Communication
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary is separated from parent AND communication is weak

actions.reason: Combination of separated living and minimal communication raises significant concerns about relationship authenticity and requires comprehensive evidence of genuine parent-child connection

questionSelectionRules.name: Age Eligibility - Near Maximum Age Requirement
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary is 19, 20, or 21 years old (near IR-2 age limit)

actions.reason: Age near eligibility limit requires careful verification that beneficiary meets all IR-2 requirements before aging out, plus documentation of relationship establishment before age 18

questionSelectionRules.name: Young Beneficiary Child
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary is young (under 14 years old)

actions.reason: Younger children typically have stronger family bonds and clearer family reunification intent; focus on parent-child relationship quality and household integration

questionSelectionRules.name: Custody Verification - Other Parent Consent
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary was not living with petitioner (requires verification of other parent consent)

actions.reason: If not living with petitioner, must verify that other custodial parent has consented to child's emigration and there are no custody/guardianship conflicts preventing IR-2 visa issuance

questionSelectionRules.name: Strong Relationship - Multiple Communication Channels
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent and child use multiple communication methods

actions.reason: Multiple communication methods demonstrate technological connectivity, accessibility, and diverse ways to maintain relationship quality

questionSelectionRules.name: Limited Communication Channel
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent and child use only one communication method

actions.reason: Single communication method may indicate limited access to technology or connectivity; does not negate relationship but requires attention to relationship maintenance

questionSelectionRules.name: Clear Immigration Intent - Articulated Reasons
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when beneficiary has provided thoughtful reasons for moving to USA

actions.reason: Clear articulation of reasons and plans demonstrates thoughtfulness and genuine intent for family reunification

questionSelectionRules.name: Petitioner - Father
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when father is the petitioner

actions.reason: Father as petitioner may require additional household and living arrangement verification to confirm child will be properly cared for

questionSelectionRules.name: Petitioner - Mother
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when mother is the petitioner

actions.reason: Mother as petitioner typically provides standard assessment of parent-child relationship and household arrangements

questionSelectionRules.name: Petitioner - Step-parent
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when step-parent is the petitioner

actions.reason: Step-parent petition requires verification of step-parent marriage before beneficiary's 18th birthday and detailed assessment of step-parent-child relationship quality

questionSelectionRules.name: Petitioner - Adoptive Parent
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when adoptive parent is the petitioner

actions.reason: Adoptive petition requires legal adoption documents, verification that adoption occurred before beneficiary's 16th birthday, and detailed relationship history
